text.title: C# Roslyn Analyzers as Compile Errors + Public API Lock Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: Repo MUST commit a top-level .editorconfig that escalates CS8602/CS8618/CS8625 (nullable), CA2007 (ConfigureAwait), VSTHRD100 (async-void), VSTHRD002 (sync-on-async), CA2016 (CancellationToken) to error severity.
rule.rationale: Analyzer at warning severity is invisible to an LLM agent; at error it becomes a compile failure the agent must fix.

rule.statement: MSBuild MUST set TreatWarningsAsErrors=true at least for Release configuration; Debug may keep warnings warnings.
rule.rationale: Errors block CI; warnings are ignored. The agent only respects errors.

rule.statement: Agents (and humans) are forbidden from `#pragma warning disable` or `[SuppressMessage]` without a same-line justification comment.
rule.rationale: Silent suppressions accumulate; named justifications make them reviewable.

rule.statement: Every published library MUST add Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.PublicApiAnalyzers + commit non-empty PublicAPI.Shipped.txt + PublicAPI.Unshipped.txt; RS0016/RS0017 at error.
rule.rationale: Public API drift breaks consumers; analyzer reduces drift to a compile failure with the exact symbol.

rule.statement: CI MUST run `dotnet build -c Release` with TreatWarningsAsErrors and fail the PR on any compile error.
rule.rationale: Local build can be skipped; CI gate is the merge floor.

rule.statement: If .NET Framework < 4.7.2 OR code generators dominate (false-positive flood) OR repo is throwaway, STOP.
rule.rationale: Forcing escalations on legacy / generator-heavy projects creates noise without value.
